name: New Fortress Energy Inc.

profile.description: New Fortress Energy Inc. operates as an integrated gas-to-power energy infrastructure company that provides energy and development services to end-users worldwide. The company operates in two segments, Terminals and Infrastructure, and Ships. The Terminals and Infrastructure segment engages in the natural gas procurement and liquefaction; and logistics, shipping, facilities and conversion, or development of natural gas-fired power generation. The Ships segment offers floating storage and regasification units (FRSU) and liquefied natural gas (LNG) carriers which are leased to customers under long-term or spot arrangements. The company operates landed micro-fuel handling facility in San Juan, Puerto Rico; and LNG receiving facility and gas-fired power plant at the Port of Pichilingue in Baja California Sur, Mexico. New Fortress Energy Inc. was founded in 1998 and is based in New York, New York.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): NFE built LNG infrastructure in Brazil and global markets, but financial distress has overshadowed operational progress. AI Deployment (0/2): None. Market Share (1/2): Meaningful LNG infrastructure position in Brazil with Barcarena terminal and power plants [1]. Pipeline (1/2): $470M contracted EBITDA from Brazil operations expected. R&D Intensity (1/1): Infrastructure technology investments above sector average.

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Providing clean energy to developing markets. Community Impact (1/2): Energy access in underserved markets. Employee Retention (0/2): No data. Insider Buying (0/2): Founder Wes Edens controlling stake but company in distress.

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. Q1 2026 revenue plummeted 51.9% to $227M with a net loss of $400M, widening from $178M a year ago [1]. Total debt of $8.3B against stockholders' deficit of $55M creates an existentially stressed balance sheet. Cash of $189.9M. The company secured UK court approval for debt-to-equity restructuring and raised $885M in secured notes [1]. Financial distress overshadows operational progress in Brazil.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): LNG infrastructure — terminals, power plants, pipelines — are physical assets that AI cannot replicate [1]. Energy transportation and distribution require massive physical infrastructure, regulatory approvals, and specialized engineering. AI demand increases electricity and LNG demand. AI Opportunity (0/1): No AI revenue.

infinite_game.summary: NFE was built on a genuine vision of bringing LNG to developing markets but financial distress from overleveraging suggests finite game capital allocation decisions overwhelmed the purpose.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): Clean energy access for developing markets is meaningful but subordinated to financial engineering.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (0/2): Financial distress creates organizational instability.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Competes with major LNG providers.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Ambitious LNG infrastructure buildout in challenging markets.

investment_thesis: NFE has $470M in contracted EBITDA from Brazil operations (Barcarena terminal, power plants, TGS terminal) providing a path to recovery if debt restructuring succeeds [1]. The company raised $885M in secured notes for Brazil operations and obtained UK court approval for debt-to-equity conversion. LNG demand growth from developing markets and AI-driven electricity needs provide secular tailwinds.
thesis_continued: NFE is in financial distress — Q1 2026 revenue fell 51.9%, net loss widened to $400M, total debt of $8.3B exceeds assets (stockholders' deficit of $55M) [1]. The debt-to-equity restructuring will massively dilute existing shareholders. Cash of only $189.9M provides minimal runway. At $0.56/share, the stock prices in near-zero equity value for existing shareholders.
key_catalysts: Debt-to-equity restructuring completion stabilizing the balance sheet
Brazil LNG infrastructure generating $470M contracted EBITDA
LNG demand growth from developing markets and AI data center power needs
key_risks: Debt restructuring massively diluting existing shareholders to near-zero recovery
Revenue decline of 51.9% continuing as LNG operations underperform contracted expectations
Cash of only $189.9M insufficient to fund operations during restructuring transition
sources: New Fortress Energy Q1 2026 10-Q and Earnings, May 2026
Yahoo Finance — NFE Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
